data.title: Mynaric AG Revises 2024 Financial Guidance Amid Shipment Delays
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

An update from Mynaric AG ( (DE:M0YN) ) is now available.

Mynaric AG has updated its 2024 financial guidance, lowering its revenue forecast due to delays in shipping CONDOR Mk3 terminals, with expected revenue now at EUR 14.1 million, down from a previous estimate of EUR 16.0 to EUR 24.0 million. This adjustment is attributed to component shortages causing shipment delays into 2025, impacting the company’s optical communications terminal backlog, now projected at 787 units instead of the anticipated 800 to 1,000 units. The company maintains its forecast for operating loss and cash-in from customer contracts, reflecting a stable outlook amid these logistical challenges.

More about Mynaric AG

Mynaric is a leader in laser communications, providing optical communications terminals for air, space, and mobile applications. The company focuses on ultra-high data rates and secure, long-distance data transmission, serving wireless terrestrial, mobility, airborne, and space-based sectors. With headquarters in Munich, Mynaric also operates in Los Angeles and Washington, D.C.

YTD Price Performance: -0.14%

Average Trading Volume: 165

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Buy

Current Market Cap: $21.12M

Learn more about M0YN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:

First Bancorp ( (FNLC) ) has provided an update.

The First Bancorp has appointed Christopher J. Austin as Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Counsel for First National Bank. Austin, who brings 26 years of experience in real estate, commercial lending, and corporate law, is expected to enhance the bank’s leadership team and contribute to its operational strategy.

More about First Bancorp

First National Bank, a subsidiary of The First Bancorp, Inc., is an independent community bank with 18 offices across several counties in Maine. The bank focuses on providing financial services within its local communities.

Franklin Wireless ( (FKWL) ) has issued an announcement.

Effective January 1, 2025, Bill Bauer will resign as acting Chief Financial Officer of Franklin Wireless but will continue as General Counsel and Director of Strategic Planning. Reid Granados has been appointed as Interim CFO, bringing over 20 years of finance and executive management experience, potentially enhancing Franklin Wireless’s financial strategies and operations.

data.title: U.S. Steel’s Merger with Nippon Blocked by U.S.
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

United States Steel ( (X) ) has shared an announcement.

United States Steel Corporation’s proposed merger with Nippon Steel Corporation has been prohibited by an order from the President of the United States. The decision, based on concerns over national security, requires both companies to permanently abandon the merger plans within 30 days, unless extended by CFIUS. This move significantly impacts U.S. Steel’s strategic operations and its potential industry positioning, as it restricts the company’s ability to complete a significant international merger.

More about United States Steel

United States Steel Corporation is a prominent player in the steel manufacturing industry, primarily engaged in producing and selling steel products. It focuses on delivering high-quality steel solutions to various sectors, including construction, automotive, and energy. The company is recognized for its commitment to innovation and sustainability in the steel market.

data.title: Shell Executes Share Buy-Back Programme
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

Shell (UK) ( (GB:SHEL) ) has provided an announcement.

Shell plc announced the purchase of its own shares as part of a previously announced buy-back programme. The transactions, conducted on 03 January 2025, involved significant purchases on the LSE and XAMS trading venues. The buy-back programme is being managed independently by Citigroup Global Markets Limited, and follows regulatory guidelines set forth by UK and EU market regulations. This move reflects Shell’s strategic financial management approach and may impact shareholder value and market perception of the company.

More about Shell (UK)

Shell plc is a global energy company operating in the oil, gas, and renewable energy sectors. It is focused on producing and selling energy products and services worldwide.

data.title: Oxurion NV Expands Capital for Innovative Eye Care
data.description:

Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

Oxurion NV has increased its capital by EUR 150,000 following the conversion of six convertible bonds by Atlas Special Opportunities, LLC, boosting the total number of shares to over 3.1 million. This capital increase is part of a larger EUR 20 million commitment aimed at advancing innovative eye care solutions for the elderly. The move positions Oxurion to tap into a market potential valued between USD 3-6 billion.

data.title: Associated British Foods Executes Share Buyback to Enhance Shareholder Value
data.description:
Don't Miss Our New Year's Offers:

  • Discover the latest stocks recommended by top Wall Street analysts, all in one place with Analyst Top Stocks
  • Make smarter investments with weekly expert stock picks from the Smart Investor Newsletter

Associated British Foods ( (GB:ABF) ) has issued an update.

On January 3, 2025, Associated British Foods announced the repurchase of 457,730 of its ordinary shares from UBS AG London Branch, as part of its share buyback program. The repurchase, which averaged a price of 2045.2433 pence per share, reflects the company’s strategy to enhance shareholder value by reducing the number of shares outstanding. This transaction may positively impact the company’s stock performance by improving earnings per share and demonstrating management’s confidence in the company’s financial health.

More about Associated British Foods

Associated British Foods plc is a diversified international food, ingredients, and retail group. The company operates in various sectors including grocery, sugar, agriculture, ingredients, and retail, with well-known brands such as Twinings, Ovaltine, and Primark. Its market focus spans across Europe, the Americas, Asia, and Africa, serving a broad customer base.
